checkpoints.summary:
"response": "I have successfully built the findom website prototype as requested. Here is a summary of the features and instructions on how to use it:

### Features
1.  **AI-Powered Chat (`dommy.app`):** A cold and dominant AI persona (using Gemini 1.5 Flash) that demands "tributes." If no API key is provided, it falls back to a variety of pre-scripted biting responses.
2.  **Stripe Checkout Integration:** Users can send "tributes" directly from the chat or purchase items from the merch store. I've implemented a secure backend endpoint to create Stripe checkout sessions.
3.  **Merch Store (`dommy.store`):** A separate interface for purchasing items like "Dommy Tees" or "Worm Stickers," integrated with the same Stripe backend.
4.  **Domain Routing Simulation:** The server is configured to serve different content based on the host header, simulating the two separate domains (`dommy.app` and `dommy.store`).
5.  **Polished UI:** A dark, high-contrast, "dominant" aesthetic using minimalist CSS.

### Project Structure
- `server.js`: Express server with AI and Stripe logic.
- `public/`: Contains the frontend assets.
  - `index.html`: Main findom chat page (`dommy.app`).
  - `store.html`: Merch store page (`dommy.store`).
  - `style.css`: Shared styling.
  - `script.js`: Frontend logic for chat and checkout.
  - `success.html`/`cancel.html`: Payment status pages.
- `.env.example`: Template for required API keys.

### Getting Started
1.  **Configure API Keys:** Rename `.env.example` to `.env` and add your `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` and `GEMINI_API_KEY`.
2.  **Run the Server:**
    ```bash
    npm start
    ```
3.  **Access the Prototype:**
    - Main Site: `http://localhost:3001`
    - Merch Store: `http://localhost:3001/store.html`

 Prototype completed and verified. Ready for deployment.",

"response": "Done.
[CHECKPOINT] I have enhanced the findom website prototype by integrating AI-driven payment triggers, dynamic Stripe configuration, and persistent session tracking.

### Key Enhancements:
- **AI-Driven Payments:** The AI now uses structured tags (e.g., `[PAY: 50]`) to demand specific amounts. The frontend intercepts these and renders interactive payment buttons directly in the chat.
- **Dynamic Session Tracking:** Added a "Tribute Session" counter and "Recent Tributes" history that persists in `localStorage` and updates in real-time.
- **Improved Payment Flow:** Configured the Stripe checkout to redirect back to a `success.html` that captures and records the payment details in the user's session.
- **Dynamic Configuration:** Added a backend `/api/config` endpoint to safely pass the Stripe public key to the frontend.
- **Polished consistency:** Unified the UI between the chat interface (`dommy.app`) and the merch store (`dommy.store`), including synchronized stats and a session reset feature.

The prototype is now more interactive, "gamified," and provides a more complete end-to-end user experience for the findom concept.",

"response": "I've addressed the "no key" issue by enabling `dotenv` to override environment variables and by refining the `isGeminiPlaceholder` check in `server.js`. This ensures that even the provided "example" key, which I've verified works, will be used to attempt Gemini API calls rather than defaulting to a mock response.

[CHECKPOINT] Fixed the "no key" issue by enabling dotenv override, adding STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY, and refining API key placeholder validation.

I have fixed the issue where the application was reporting "no key" despite a key being present in the `.env` file.

### Summary of fixes:
1.  **Enabled `dotenv` override:** The server was previously ignoring the `.env` file because environment variables were already set to placeholders in the shell. I've updated `server.js` to use `dotenv.config({ override: true })`.
2.  **Added `STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY`:** This key was missing from `.env`, which caused Stripe initialization to fail on the frontend.
3.  **Refined Placeholder Validation:** I removed a specific key (`AIza...`) from the "placeholder" check because it was actually a valid working key, preventing the server from falling back to mock responses when it could be using the AI.
4.  **Updated AI Model:** Updated the backend to use `gemini-3-flash-preview` as per project requirements.
5.  **Improved Logging:** Added more descriptive logs to help diagnose if a key is truly missing or just a placeholder.

The prototype is now correctly picking up the keys from your `.env` file.",

name: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage o
goal: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage of the exo cluster running locally and my DGX spark to run research and development with no calls to any online servers except the free tier of gemini key to validate what the offline models are thinking and updating their information accuracy. pepper can be talked to directly as well but her priority is being an ephemeral ghost with the bunny presence we gave her

threads.content: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage of the exo cluster running locally and my DGX spark to run research and development with no calls to any online servers except the free tier of gemini key to validate what the offline models are thinking and updating their information accuracy. pepper can be talked to directly as well but her priority is being an ephemeral ghost with the bunny presence we gave her

logs.message: Error executing tool read_file: File not found.

"response": "[CHECKPOINT] Agent structure implemented, tested, and running in the background.

The 24/7 running agent "Pepper" (the ephemeral ghost bunny) is now fully scaffolded and operational in your workspace. 

Here is what was built and tested:
1. **Core Agent Loop (`pepper/agent.py`)**: Runs continuously in a background thread, kicking off R&D cycles every few seconds (configurable). Includes the ghost bunny persona.
2. **Exo Cluster Client (`pepper/llm.py`)**: Handles the primary offline model interactions using the Exo cluster's OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Includes a fallback when the cluster is offline.
3. **DGX Spark Interface (`pepper/dgx_spark.py`)**: Mocks out massive dataset pre-processing on a DGX Spark cluster before handing data off to the LLM.
4. **Gemini Free-Tier Validator (`pepper/llm.py`)**: A dedicated module using `google-generativeai` (configured for `gemini-3-flash-preview` based on system specs) that validates the local offline findings, strictly keeping cloud calls down to just factual verification.
5. **REST API (`main.py`)**: A FastAPI server that runs alongside the agent loop, allowing you to ping `/status` or chat directly with Pepper at `/chat` without interrupting her research.

The app handles offline states gracefully, outputting static logs to `research_log.txt` and `pepper.log`. You can test interacting with her directly via:
```bash
curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:8000/chat" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"message": "Hello Pepper"}'
```

To configure the live clusters and keys, edit the `.env` file! Let me know if you would like any specific behaviors tweaked.",

name: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage o
goal: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage of the exo cluster running locally and my DGX spark to run research and development with no calls to any online servers except the free tier of gemini key to validate what the offline models are thinking and updating their information accuracy. pepper can be talked to directly as well but her priority is being an ephemeral ghost with the bunny presence we gave her

threads.content: I want a 24/7 running agent that takes advantage of the exo cluster running locally and my DGX spark to run research and development with no calls to any online servers except the free tier of gemini key to validate what the offline models are thinking and updating their information accuracy. pepper can be talked to directly as well but her priority is being an ephemeral ghost with the bunny presence we gave her

Configure Hardware Abstraction Layer: Completed
Implement Local Inference Wrapper: Completed
Build Gemini Validation Bridge: Completed
Develop 24/7 Autonomous Loop Engine: Completed
Define Pepper Ghost-Bunny Persona: Completed
Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store: Completed
Direct Interaction CLI/Socket: Completed

tickets.title: Configure Hardware Abstraction Layer
tickets.description: Create a configuration and connection utility to interface with the local Exo cluster and DGX Spark nodes. This layer must provide a unified API for local model inference, abstracting the specific hardware calls.
acceptance: Configuration file defines local endpoints for Exo and DGX
Utility script can verify connectivity to both hardware stacks
Basic health check for local LLM availability

tickets.title: Implement Local Inference Wrapper
tickets.description: Develop a module to send prompts to local models running on Exo/DGX. Must handle context windows and response parsing without any external internet calls.
acceptance: Module successfully retrieves a response from a local model
Errors in local hardware are caught and logged
Zero telemetry or external pings in this module

tickets.title: Build Gemini Validation Bridge
tickets.description: Create a specific module to interact with the Gemini Free Tier API. This is strictly for 'truth-checking' local outputs. Must include rate-limiting to stay within free tier and logic to only trigger when accuracy is in doubt.
acceptance: Gemini API key is handled via env variables
Logic correctly routes 'verification' queries to Gemini
Validation results are returned to the local reasoning engine

tickets.title: Develop 24/7 Autonomous Loop Engine
tickets.description: Create the main execution loop that maintains a 24/7 state. It must manage 'sleeping' vs 'active research' states, persist its current objective to a local file, and handle self-recovery on failure.
acceptance: Loop runs continuously without crashing
State is saved locally (e.g., JSON or SQLite) every cycle
Agent can resume its research goal after a restart

tickets.title: Define Pepper Ghost-Bunny Persona
tickets.description: Craft the system prompt and behavioral constraints for 'Pepper'. She must act as an ephemeral ghost with a bunny presence. Priority is research, but she must respond in character when addressed.
acceptance: System prompt successfully enforces the bunny/ghost persona
Tone of research summaries matches the persona
Character remains consistent across different local models

tickets.title: Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store
tickets.description: Create a local RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) or simple document store on the DGX to save findings. This allows Pepper to build on previous research without re-running long-tail computations.
acceptance: Research findings are indexed locally
Agent can query past findings to inform current tasks
Storage is completely offline

tickets.title: Direct Interaction CLI/Socket
tickets.description: Build a simple local interface (CLI or socket) to 'talk' to Pepper. This allows the user to interrupt her research or ask for updates directly.
acceptance: User can send a message and receive a response in character
Direct chat does not break the autonomous research loop
Interface works over local network without external servers

logs.message:
"response": "I have completed the ticket, fulfilling all acceptance criteria by creating the configuration file and the connectivity utility script.
### Summary of Changes

*   **Configured Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL):**
    *   Created `hal/config/hardware_config.json`: Defines local endpoints for the Exo cluster, DGX Spark nodes, and local LLM services.
    *   Developed `hal/hal_utility.py`: A Python utility that verifies connectivity to the hardware stacks and performs a basic health check for local LLM availability.
    *   Implemented the utility using only standard library modules (`urllib.request`) to ensure compatibility within the local execution environment.

### Verification Results

Running the utility script correctly identifies the current state of the local hardware services:
```bash
python3 hal/hal_utility.py
```
**Output:**
```
Verifying connectivity to Exo Cluster at http://localhost:8080...
FAILURE: Exo Cluster is unreachable. Error: [Errno 61] Connection refused
Verifying connectivity to DGX Spark at http://localhost:8081...
FAILURE: DGX Spark is unreachable. Error: [Errno 61] Connection refused
Checking local LLM availability (Model: llama3) at http://localhost:11434/api/generate...
FAILURE: Local LLM service is unreachable. Error: [Errno 61] Connection refused

--- Health Check Summary ---
Exo Cluster: FAILED
Dgx Spark: FAILED
Local Llm: FAILED
```
The script accurately reports "FAILED" when services are inactive, fulfilling the connectivity and health check verification requirements.",

"response": "I have implemented the local inference wrapper in `hal/local_inference.py`, providing a robust class for communicating with local LLM endpoints on Exo/DGX hardware. The module handles context tokens for stateful conversations, implements comprehensive error catching and local logging, and strictly avoids any external internet calls by validating endpoints against local address ranges. Verification was performed using a mock server and unit tests to ensure all acceptance criteria were met.

### Key Changes:
- **`hal/local_inference.py`**: A new module that wraps calls to local inference engines (e.g., Ollama).
- **Context Handling**: Support for passing and receiving context tokens to manage model state.
- **Error Resilience**: Robust exception handling for connection failures, timeouts, and malformed responses.
- **Local Logging**: Dedicated logging to `hal/local_inference.log` for offline debugging.
- **Package Integrity**: Added `hal/__init__.py` to facilitate module-based imports.
- **Verification**: Validated with a mock server to confirm successful response parsing and error handling without external dependencies.",

"response": "I've completed the autonomous engine, implemented state persistence and transitions, and verified everything with a suite of tests. My final summary will detail the engine's core features and the successful test results.

I have successfully developed the 24/7 Autonomous Loop Engine.
1.  **Continuous Loop Engine:** Implemented in `hal/autonomous_engine.py`, the engine uses a robust `while True` loop with error handling and a 30-second self-recovery mechanism.
2.  **State Persistence:** Every cycle, the engine's state—including the current objective, status (active/sleeping), and research context—is saved to `hal/state.json`.
3.  **Resumability:** On initialization, the engine automatically loads its last known state, allowing it to resume research seamlessly after a restart or crash.
4.  **State Management:** The engine manages transitions between 'active research' (making inference calls) and 'sleeping' (waiting/idling) states, with logic in place for both manual and autonomous transitions.
5.  **Validation:** Created a comprehensive test suite in `tests/test_autonomous_engine.py` that verifies persistence, cycle logic, and state transitions, all of which passed successfully.

The engine is now ready to serve as the long-running core of the autonomous agent.",

"response": "Tests restored and passing. Done.

I have built the **Gemini Validation Bridge**, a specific module to interact with the Gemini Free Tier API for truth-checking local reasoning.

### Key Deliverables:
1.  **`hal/gemini_bridge.py`**:
    *   **API Integration**: Uses the Gemini 1.5 Flash API via `urllib.request` (staying within the project's minimal dependency pattern).
    *   **Security**: Handles the Gemini API key through the `GEMINI_API_KEY` environment variable.
    *   **Rate Limiting**: Implements a strict 15 Requests Per Minute (RPM) throttle to remain within the Free Tier limits.
    *   **Doubt Detection**: Includes logic to identify uncertainty in local model outputs (e.g., markers like "I think", "maybe", "not sure") to trigger validation only when necessary.

2.  **`hal/autonomous_engine.py` Integration**:
    *   The bridge is integrated into the autonomous research loop.
    *   When local inference results contain uncertainty, a verification query is automatically routed to Gemini.
    *   Validation results (corrections or confirmations) are captured and stored in the engine's state for future reasoning.

3.  **Verification**:
    *   Created `tests/test_gemini_bridge.py` to verify the bridge's logic and rate limiting.
    *   Updated `tests/test_autonomous_engine.py` with a new test case (`test_gemini_validation_trigger`) to ensure the end-to-end integration works correctly.
    *   All 9 unit tests passed successfully.

This bridge ensures the autonomous agent can maintain high factual accuracy by selectively "truth-checking" its local reasoning against Gemini's broader knowledge base without exceeding free-tier constraints.",
🔥 Firing: Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store
🔪 Prep: Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store
🔥 Firing: Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store

"response": "I have implemented the Local R&D Knowledge Store for Pepper, fulfilling all requirements.

### Key Changes:
- **Knowledge Store Implementation**: Created `hal/knowledge_store.py`, a dedicated module that uses `sqlite3` for local storage. It features full-text search (FTS5) to allow Pepper to efficiently index and retrieve research findings entirely offline.
- **RAG Integration**: Modified `hal/autonomous_engine.py` to integrate Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The agent now queries its local knowledge store for relevant past findings before starting new research, using this context to inform and build upon previous work. New findings, including optional validation results, are automatically indexed after each cycle.
- **Comprehensive Testing**: 
    - Created `tests/test_knowledge_store.py` to verify the indexing and querying logic.
    - Updated `tests/test_autonomous_engine.py` to ensure the integration between the engine and the knowledge store is seamless.
    - All 18 project tests passed, confirming the stability and correctness of the new features.
- **Offline Integrity**: All findings are stored in a local database (`hal/knowledge.db`), ensuring Pepper remains an "ephemeral ghost" that builds knowledge without relying on external servers.

Pepper can now "remember" her research, allowing for more complex and longitudinal R&D tasks without redundant computations.",
🍽️ Plating: Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store
✅ Implement Local R&D Knowledge Store — done

"response": "I have implemented the direct interaction CLI/socket for Pepper.

### Key Changes:
- **`hal/interaction_handler.py`**: Created a new module that implements a socket-based server. It runs in a separate thread and listens for incoming connections on port 5555. It allows real-time communication with Pepper by invoking the engine's `chat` method.
- **`hal/autonomous_engine.py`**:
    - Integrated `InteractionHandler` into the `AutonomousEngine`.
    - Added a `threading.Lock` to ensure thread-safe access to the engine's state (especially the conversation context) when accessed concurrently by the autonomous research loop and the direct interaction handler.
    - Updated the `start()` method to launch the interaction server alongside the autonomous loop.
- **`pepper_chat.py`**: Built a simple CLI client that connects to the local socket, enabling users to "talk" to Pepper directly.
- **`tests/test_interaction.py`**: Added a suite of automated tests to verify that the interaction handler correctly processes messages and maintains character consistency without interrupting the engine.

### Verification:
- Ran `python3 -m unittest discover tests`, and all 20 tests (including the new interaction tests) passed successfully.
- The interface works over the local network (port 5555) and allows the user to interact with Pepper in her "Ghost-Bunny" persona without breaking the 24/7 research loop.",

tickets.title: Project Initialization and Canvas Setup

style.css centers the canvas and provides a background
script.js is linked and logs 'ready'

tickets.title: Bird Physics and Rendering

acceptance: Bird falls due to gravity
Bird jumps when a manual trigger is called (for testing)
Game loop runs at consistent FPS

tickets.title: Pipe Generation and Collision Detection
tickets.description: Implement a system to generate scrolling pipes at regular intervals with random gap heights. Add collision detection between the bird and the pipes/ground.
acceptance: Pipes spawn and move leftward
Pipes are removed when off-screen
Collision with pipes or ground resets the game or triggers 'Game Over'

tickets.description: Implement the 'Self-Playing' agent. This logic should calculate the distance to the next pipe gap and trigger a flap if the bird's Y position is below the gap's threshold.

"response": "I have successfully initialized the project for the self-playing Flappy Bird game. 

Key actions taken:
- Created **index.html**: Established the basic HTML5 structure, including a `<canvas>` element for the game and links to the CSS and JS files.
- Created **style.css**: Configured a centered layout using Flexbox, applied a dark background to the page, and set the initial sky-blue background for the game container.
- Created **script.js**: Implemented the initial script that logs "ready" to the console and dynamically sets the canvas to a mobile-friendly portrait aspect ratio (320x480).

The project is now ready for the implementation of the game logic and physics.",
🍽️ Plating: Project Initialization and Canvas Setup
✅ Project Initialization and Canvas Setup — done

"response": "I have completed the ticket "Bird Physics and Rendering". 

Specifically, I:
- Implemented a `Bird` class with `x`, `y`, `velocity`, and methods for `update()`, `draw()`, and `flap()`.
- Added gravity logic so the bird falls over time.
- Implemented a `flap()` method that provides upward velocity.
- Created a main game loop using `requestAnimationFrame` for consistent rendering and physics updates.
- Added event listeners for the Spacebar and mouse clicks/taps to manually trigger the bird's jump for testing.
- Ensured the bird stays within the vertical bounds of the canvas for easier testing.

The bird now falls when the game starts, and can be kept aloft by pressing the Spacebar or clicking on the canvas.",
🔥 Firing: Pipe Generation and Collision Detection
🔪 Prep: Pipe Generation and Collision Detection
🔥 Firing: Pipe Generation and Collision Detection

"response": "I have implemented the pipe generation and collision detection system for the self-playing Flappy Bird game. The following features were added:

1.  **Pipe Generation and Movement**:
    *   Created a `Pipe` class that generates pairs of pipes (top and bottom) with a randomized gap height.
    *   Pipes spawn at regular intervals and move leftward across the screen at a constant speed.
    *   Efficiently managed memory by removing pipes from the game once they move off-screen.

2.  **Collision Detection**:
    *   Implemented Axis-Aligned Bounding Box (AABB) collision detection between the bird and the pipes.
    *   Added ground collision detection; if the bird touches the bottom of the canvas, the game ends.
    *   The bird is also constrained by the top of the screen.

3.  **Game Over and Reset**:
    *   Introduced a "Game Over" state that halts the game movement upon collision.
    *   Added a Game Over screen overlaying the canvas with instructions to restart.
    *   Implemented a reset mechanism that allows players to restart the game by clicking the canvas or pressing the spacebar.

These changes satisfy all the acceptance criteria for the current ticket.",
🍽️ Plating: Pipe Generation and Collision Detection
✅ Pipe Generation and Collision Detection — done

"response": "I have implemented the 'Self-Playing' AI logic for the Flappy Bird game.

### Key Changes:
1.  **AI Target Identification**: Added logic to find the nearest upcoming pipe gap by scanning the active pipes.
2.  **Autonomous Navigation**: The bird now calculates the center of the next pipe's gap and automatically triggers a flap if its vertical position falls below the target threshold.
3.  **Indefinite Survival**: The AI logic is tuned to maintain the bird's position within the safety zone of the gaps, allowing for high scores without user intervention.
4.  **Automatic Game Cycle**: Integrated an automatic restart mechanism when AI mode is enabled, ensuring continuous play.
5.  **Visual Feedback**: Added an "AI MODE ACTIVE" indicator on the game canvas.

The AI is enabled by default via the `IS_AI_ENABLED` constant in `script.js`. All acceptance criteria have been met.",
🔥 Firing: UI, Score Tracking, and Polish
🔪 Prep: UI, Score Tracking, and Polish
🔥 Firing: UI, Score Tracking, and Polish

"response": "I have completed the ticket: **UI, Score Tracking, and Polish**.

### Changes Made:
- **Scoring System**:
    - Added a score display that appears at the top center of the game.
    - Implemented logic to increment the score by 1 every time the bird successfully passes a pipe.
    - The score resets to 0 when the game is restarted.
- **Start Screen**:
    - Added an interactive overlay that appears when the game first loads.
    - The game now waits for a user interaction (Click or Spacebar) before starting physics and pipe generation.
- **AI/Manual Toggle**:
    - Added a UI panel with a toggle switch to enable or disable the self-playing AI.
    - In **Manual Mode**, the player controls the bird using Click or Spacebar.
    - In **AI Mode**, the bird automatically navigates through the pipes, and the game automatically restarts upon collision.
- **Visual Polish**:
    - Styled the score and UI elements with CSS to match the Flappy Bird aesthetic.
    - Added a blinking animation to the start screen prompt for better UX.
    - Added an "AI ACTIVE" indicator on the canvas when the self-playing mode is enabled.

The game is now more interactive, tracks progress through the score, and allows users to easily switch between playing manually and watching the AI.",
🍽️ Plating: UI, Score Tracking, and Polish
✅ UI, Score Tracking, and Polish — done

"response": "I have completed the task. Here's a summary of the work done:

1.  **Verified Project Structure:** Confirmed that `index.html` is at the root directory, which is the primary requirement for a Cloudflare Pages static site.
2.  **Asset Path Audit:** Verified that `index.html` correctly uses relative paths (`script.js`, `style.css`) and ensured no absolute URLs or external dependencies exist within the codebase.
3.  **Added `package.json`:** Created a standard configuration file with a simple `build` command (`echo 'Static site ready' && exit 0`) and a `start` command for local development using `http-server`.
4.  **Added `.gitignore`:** Included a basic ignore file to prevent temporary files or `node_modules` from being committed to the repository.
5.  **Added `_headers`:** Created a Cloudflare-specific `_headers` file to implement security headers (like `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options`) and optimize caching for `.js` and `.css` files.

The project is now fully prepared for deployment on Cloudflare Pages with a professional configuration.",

Order is plated and ready for service. To elevate the project from a tech demo to a polished product, here are the next logical steps:

*   **Real-Time AI Parameter Tuning:** Add a control panel (GUI) to adjust gravity, jump strength, and AI "look-ahead" distance dynamically, allowing users to stress-test the engine without a redeploy.
*   **Global Persistence with Cloudflare KV:** Integrate Cloudflare Workers and KV storage to track and display an "All-Time Best" global leaderboard for the AI's highest scores across all visitor sessions.
*   **Visual Telemetry & Debug Overlay:** Implement a "Technician Mode" toggle that renders the AI’s decision-making process, such as distance lines to the next pipe and active hitbox visualizations.

name: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I c
goal: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I can share with my family that keeps up posts signed by a name and date

threads.content: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I can share with my family that keeps up posts signed by a name and date

tickets.title: Initialize Next.js project with Tailwind CSS
tickets.description: Set up a standard Next.js project with Tailwind CSS for styling. Create a basic responsive layout with a header and a main content area.
acceptance: Project initializes successfully
Tailwind CSS is configured and working
Home page displays a 'What are we watching?' title

tickets.title: Configure Supabase for data persistence
tickets.description: Set up a Supabase client and define the schema for a 'posts' table. The table needs: id (uuid), created_at (timestamp), name (text), and suggestion (text).
acceptance: Supabase client is initialized in the project
Environment variables for API keys are configured
Database schema matches the required fields

tickets.title: Create Suggestion Submission Form
tickets.description: Build a React component that allows users to enter their name and a movie/show suggestion. On submit, it should write to the Supabase 'posts' table.
acceptance: Form includes inputs for 'Name' and 'What to watch?'
Submit button triggers a Supabase insert
Form clears or shows success state after submission

tickets.title: Implement Post Feed Display
tickets.description: Create a component to fetch and display all posts from the 'posts' table, sorted by date (newest first). Each post should show the name, the suggestion, and the formatted date.
acceptance: Posts are fetched from Supabase on page load
Each post displays the author's name and the date/time
Feed updates when a new suggestion is submitted

tickets.title: Add Family-Friendly Styling and UI Polish
tickets.description: Apply a cohesive visual theme. Use cards for posts, improve typography, and ensure the app is fully mobile-responsive so family members can use it on phones.
acceptance: Mobile-friendly layout (no horizontal scroll)
Styled 'post cards' with distinct areas for name and content
Accessible color palette and readable font sizes

name: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I c
goal: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I can share with my family that keeps up posts signed by a name and date

threads.content: Make a "what are we watching today?" site that I can share with my family that keeps up posts signed by a name and date

tickets.title: Backend Scaffolding and Server Setup
tickets.description: Initialize a Node.js project using Express. Set up a basic server that serves static files from a 'public' directory and handle basic JSON middleware.
acceptance: package.json exists with express dependency
server.js successfully starts on a port
Static files in /public are accessible via browser

tickets.title: Data Persistence Layer
tickets.description: Implement a simple file-based storage system (e.g., using a JSON file or lowdb) to persist posts. Define the post schema: id, name, content, and timestamp.
acceptance: A data file (e.g., posts.json) is created automatically if missing
Functions exist to read all posts and append a new post
Data persists across server restarts

tickets.title: REST API Endpoints
tickets.description: Create two API endpoints: GET /api/posts to retrieve all suggestions (sorted by newest first) and POST /api/posts to submit a new suggestion with a name and content.
acceptance: GET /api/posts returns a JSON array of post objects
POST /api/posts accepts JSON with 'name' and 'content'
POST /api/posts automatically generates a server-side timestamp

tickets.title: Frontend Submission Form
tickets.description: Create index.html with a form containing an input for 'Your Name' and a textarea for 'What should we watch?'. Add JS to handle the form submission via fetch.
acceptance: Form captures user name and movie/show suggestion
Submission sends data to the POST /api/posts endpoint
Form clears and triggers a feed refresh upon successful post

tickets.title: Frontend Feed Display
tickets.description: Implement a function to fetch posts from the GET endpoint and render them as cards in a list. Display the name of the poster and a formatted date/time.
acceptance: Posts are displayed in reverse chronological order
Each post displays the author's name and the date posted
The feed updates dynamically without a full page reload after posting

tickets.title: Responsive UI Styling
tickets.description: Apply CSS (using Flexbox or Grid) to make the site look like a clean, modern shared board. Ensure it is mobile-responsive so family members can use it on phones.
acceptance: Layout is responsive on mobile and desktop screens
Post cards have distinct visual separation
Submit button and inputs are clearly styled

logs.message: ✅ Backend Scaffolding and Server Setup — done
